name: ocean
description: ocean basin location where the study took place

name: study location
description: specific location where the study took place, e.g., name of bay, reef, island, and country

name: study site
description: categorical variable for whether study was in a field or laboratory

name: study type
description: categorical variable for manipulative or observational experiment

name: event latitude
description: If there was a single stressor event, as with dredging or mining, etc., what was the location in latitude? If there was no single stressor event, write n/a.

name: event longitude
description: If there was a single stressor event, as with dredging or mining, etc., what was the location in longitude? If there was no single stressor event, write n/a.

name: num responses
description: number of response outcome types recorded in paper. This should be the length of the \"responses\" list in each setup. Each setup should have the same set of responses.

name: num setups
description: number of setups recorded in paper, where one setup is a unique set of coral genus and species, sediment composition, sediment level, and sediment exposure duration. This should be the length of the "setups" list

name: num species
description: Number of different species (<genus> <species>) examined in this paper

name: coral age class
description: categorical variable describing the broad age class of the coral that was used in the focal reference; GAMETES - eggs and/or sperm, LARVA - pre-settlement or at settlement, JUVENILE - post-settlement, ADULT - older individuals not designated as juveniles in the text (most cases)

name: current genus name
description: genus name of focal species

name: current species name
description: species name of focal species

name: colony form
possible vals: massive, palmate, plating, ramose, branching, submassive, cup, encrusting, flabellate, foliaceous, free-living, phaceloid, etc
description: categorical variable for the morphological form that the coral colony takes

name: sediment composition
description: brief description of substrate type and size, if analyzed; note if sediment was dried

name: sediment level
description: numeric value describing level of exposure to sediment; if a range (e.g., X - Y) is reported instead of a mean, then report the midpoint of the range here

name: sediment level UoM
description: unit of measurement (UoM) corresponding to sediment level (most often mg/L or NTU for suspended sediment or mg/cm2/d for deposited sediment)

name: sediment level converted to mg/cm2/day
description: sediment level converted to mg/cm2/day (if already in this UoM, then REPEAT the sediment level here)

name: sediment level converted to mg/L
description: sediment level converted to mg/L (if already in this UoM, then REPEAT the sediment level here)

name: sediment level average type
possible vals: absolute, mean, median, mode, range, etc
description: categorical variable for type of average reported for the sediment level; e.g., absolute, mean, median, mode, range, etc.; if range, see special instructions in note for "Sediment level"

name: sediment N for computing average
description: sample size used for computing average of sediment level; if N is reported as a range instead of an exact sample size by treatment, then put the lower end of the range here and make a note of the reported range in the notes at the end of this section

name: sediment N UoM
possible vals: sediment traps for deposited sediment, etc
description: unit of measurement corresponding to sediment N

name: lower error
description: lower estimate of error for reported sediment level, e.g., if reported 0.7+/-0.1, then enter '0.1' here (NOT 0.6, the lower error 'bound'); if a range (e.g., X - Y) is reported instead of a mean, then the lower error estimate = 'sediment level' - X (where 'sediment level' is the midpoint of the reported range)

name: upper error
description: upper estimate of error for reported sediment level, e.g., if reported 0.7+/-0.1, then enter '0.1' here (NOT 0.8, the upper error 'bound'); if a range (e.g., X - Y) is reported instead of a mean, then the 'upper error estimate' = Y - 'sediment level' (where 'sediment level' is the midpoint of the reported range)

name: error type
description: type of error for reported sediment level; see QUICK KEY for abbreviations ("s.e." for standard error of the mean, "s.d." for standard deviation, "95% CI" for 95% confidence interval

name: exposure duration
description: numeric value of duration of time that coral was exposed to sediment level

name: duration unit
description: unit of measurement corresponding to exposure duration

name: duration (days)
description: numeric value of duration of time that coral was exposed to sediment level in # of days

name: notes
description: any commentary concerning sediment stressor, including Figure/Table/Text from which information was taken

name: High yield/ chlA?
description: binary variable for whether or not this condition/treatment/control found increased yield or chlorophyll A content with respect to the control/ambient conditions; LEAVE BLANK if not reported or mentioned (do NOT write n/r, n/a), control is always "0" (no), and treatment is either "0" or "1" (yes)

name: Reduced P/R?
description: binary variable for whether or not this condition/treatment/control found reduced photosynthesis/respiration ratios with respect to the control/ambient conditions

name: Reduced photosynthetic efficiency?
description: binary variable for whether or not this condition/treatment/control found reduced photosynthetic efficiency with respect to the control/ambient conditions

name: Reduced growth rate?
description: binary variable for whether or not this condition/treatment/control found reduced growth rate with respect to the control/ambient conditions

name: Death of colonies?
description: binary variable for whether or not this condition/treatment/control found death of whole colonies with respect to the control/ambient conditions

name: source
description: any notes on from where binary data were derived

name: Non-adverse effect reported?
description: binary variable specifying whether the study reported binary data for non-adverse effects of sediment on coral under specified condition/treatment

name: Adverse effect reported?
description: binary variable specifying whether the study reported binary data for adverse effects of sediment on coral under specified condition/treatment

name: Mortality reported?
description: binary variable specifying whether the study reported binary data for mortality of any age class of coral (partial or total) under specified condition/treatment

name: Adult mortality reported?
description: binary variable specifying whether the study reported binary data for mortality of adult corals only (partial or total) under specified condition/treatment

name: response type
possible vals: Bleaching, Calcification rate, Chlorophyll concentration, Growth rate, Mortality rate, Mucus Production, Photosynthesis, Respiration, etc
description: categorical variable that broadly describes the type of measured response by the coral; suggested response types listed, but this list is not exhaustive -- you can write-in other response

name: level
description: numeric value describing coral's response to condition/treatment

name: unit
description: unit of measurement corresponding to response level

name: level type
possible vals: absolute/raw, mean, median, mode, range, etc.
description: categorical variable for type of average reported for the response

name: sample size (n)
description: sample size used for computing average of response; if N is reported as a range instead of an exact sample size by treatment, then put the lower end of the range here and make a note of the reported range in the notes at the end of this section

name: n unit
description: unit of measurement corresponding to response N

name: time to response, numeric
description: numeric value indicating amount of time elapsed between start of stressor and start of response

name: time to response, UoM
description: unit of measurement corresponding to amount of time elapsed between start of stressor and start of response

name: source
description: Figure, Table, or location in text from which these data were found in the the focal reference and any other notes on this response
